categories.adversary_capability: Privileged operator or compromised admin account modifies ledger files or database rows outside enforced append-only interfaces.
categories.mitigation: Append-only hash-chained storage, least-privilege RBAC, separation of duties, external copies of exports, integrity verification endpoints.
categories.out_of_scope: Insider with unfettered OS access who disables all controls simultaneously; supply-chain replacement of the entire runtime.
categories.residual_risk: Break-glass procedures and offline backups can still be abused if policy is weak.

categories.adversary_capability: Attacker deletes or truncates evidence logs, bundles, or export archives to hide non-compliance.
categories.mitigation: Immutable or WORM storage where deployed, replicated exports to independent systems, hash checkpoints, off-site backups.
categories.out_of_scope: Recovery of evidence never ingested; protection when the deployer refuses all external replication.
categories.residual_risk: If no independent copy exists, deletion may be undetectable beyond absence of records.

categories.adversary_capability: Model provider and deploying organisation collude to omit unfavourable evaluations or incidents from submitted evidence.
categories.mitigation: Third-party monitoring, customer-controlled keys, independent attestation, regulatory access to raw telemetry outside vendor-only channels.
categories.out_of_scope: Cryptographic proof of model weights or hidden provider behaviour without agreed attestation APIs.
categories.residual_risk: Collusion undermines any voluntary disclosure regime.

categories.adversary_capability: API keys, JWT signing material, or database credentials exfiltrated; attacker forges evidence or verdict reads.
categories.mitigation: Short-lived credentials, HSM/KMS where available, rotation, anomaly detection, mTLS between CI and audit service.
categories.out_of_scope: Endpoint compromise that exfiltrates plaintext after legitimate decryption.
categories.residual_risk: Stolen active sessions may still submit events until revocation.

categories.adversary_capability: Withholding evidence submission so runs remain BLOCKED or ambiguous indefinitely.
categories.mitigation: Policy timeouts, escalation workflows, contractual audit rights, fail-closed defaults documented in trust model.
categories.out_of_scope: Forcing a vendor to produce proprietary telemetry they legally cannot export.
categories.residual_risk: Organisational deadlock is a process problem, not solely technical.

categories.adversary_capability: Fabricated metrics, synthetic benchmarks, or misleading model cards supplied as evidence events.
categories.mitigation: Signed attestations where available, cross-checks against independent probes, human review gates, anomaly rules on ingest.
categories.out_of_scope: Semantic guarantee that natural-language claims are truthful.
categories.residual_risk: High-quality false data may pass structural checks.

categories.adversary_capability: Partial evidence set satisfies schema but omits decisive incidents or populations.
categories.mitigation: Explicit required-evidence model, discovery-driven requirements, BLOCKED semantics until prerequisites met.
categories.out_of_scope: Detection of unknown unknowns never instrumented in the first place.
categories.residual_risk: Policy may be under-specified relative to real harm scenarios.

categories.adversary_capability: Provider refuses model lineage, safety benchmark, or usage metadata needed for policy.
categories.mitigation: Fallback strategies (see docs/standards/fallback-strategies.md), contractual minimums, open-weights alternatives, regulatory pressure.
categories.out_of_scope: Extracting trade secrets absent legal authority or cooperation.
categories.residual_risk: Some deployments cannot reach VALID without vendor cooperation.

description: Structured threat rows aligned with GovAI research documentation; illustrative, not exhaustive.
